title: Capacity of Multi-Head Systems
concept: Multi-head attention allows the model to build rich, multi-faceted representations of word context. Boss explain ends with: 'Next up: Positional Encoding.'

exercises.prompt: How does a basic attention mechanism help a model understand a word?

options.text: By looking at the surrounding words for context
By translating the word into another language instantly
By deleting the word from the sentence

exercises.explain: Attention helps a model understand a word by looking at the other words around it for context.

cards.title: One Picture, Many Eyes
cards.body: If you look at a painting, you might focus on the vibrant colors. Someone else might notice the brushstrokes, and a third person might look at the facial expressions. In AI, a single attention head can only focus on one pattern at a time. What if we could use multiple heads to see the whole picture?

cards.title: Multi-Head Capacity
cards.body: In Multi-Head Attention, we don't just run one attention calculator. Instead, we run several independent 'heads' side-by-side. Each head is free to focus on different clues, giving the system a massive capacity to build rich, multi-faceted representations of how words connect.

cards.title: The Judge Panel
cards.body: Imagine a panel of five judges evaluating a dance performance. One judge focuses only on the footwork, another watches the dancers' hand expressions, while a third tracks the rhythm. By combining their specialized reports, the panel gets a complete picture of the dance that no single judge could capture alone.

cards.title: Two Connections at Once
cards.body: In the sentence 'The bank of the river was wet,' one attention head might connect 'bank' to 'river' (identifying the physical location). At the same time, another head might connect 'bank' to 'wet' (learning about its state). Both meanings are kept and combined!

cards.title: Are Heads Just Copies?
cards.body: Many believe that adding more attention heads just makes the model repeat the same work faster. In reality, the heads naturally specialize. They learn to look for completely different relationships, such as grammar patterns, pronouns, or physical locations!

cards.title: Single vs. Multi-Head
cards.body: Compare how a single-head system limits your perspective versus how a multi-head system expands it.

left.text: Can only focus on one type of relationship at a time (e.g., just finding pronouns).

right.text: Simultaneously tracks grammar, description, action, and logic across many heads.

exercises.prompt: What is the main benefit of using multiple attention heads instead of just one?

options.text: It can focus on different types of word relationships at the same time.
It runs much slower and uses less data.
It forces every head to look at the exact same word connection.
It deletes the words to save computer memory.

exercises.explain: Multi-head attention lets the model look at the same word from multiple perspectives (like grammar, logic, and context) simultaneously.

exercises.prompt: True or False: A multi-head attention system can capture both grammar relationships and factual connections at the same time.
exercises.explain: Multi-head attention allows different heads to focus on different aspects of a sentence at the same time.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the sentence.
exercises.sentence: Using multiple attention heads increases the model's capacity to represent complex word meanings.
exercises.explain: By splitting the attention mechanism into multiple heads, the model expands its capacity to capture different word relationships at the same time.

exercises.prompt: Which of these sounds like a Multi-Head Attention system at work?

options.text: A computer program that copies the exact same sentence ten times in a row.
An AI analyzing a sentence where Head A tracks the verbs, while Head B tracks the nouns.
A single calculator adding up a column of numbers one by one.

exercises.explain: The human analyst represents AI behavior here, because multiple heads look for different specific patterns (grammar, subject, action) in the text simultaneously.

exercises.prompt: Why does a multi-head attention system have a higher capacity for understanding than a single-head system?

options.text: It makes the model process words one by one, slowly.
It allows the model to capture multiple different relationships at once.
It reduces the total number of words the model can read.
It deletes word contexts to save memory space.

exercises.explain: Multi-head attention acts like a team of specialized readers working on the same text. By having multiple heads, the model can capture different types of relationships simultaneously, maximizing its learning capacity! Next up: Positional Encoding.
